column2: What display symptom category is represented by this pattern, and what should a technician avoid assuming?
column4: Display artifacts or distortion. Do not assume only the monitor failed; test the cable, display, video adapter, and driver path.
column6:
Troubleshooting Logic

Lines, blocks, and flashing color patterns can come from several parts of the display path. The visual clue should trigger isolation, not instant monitor replacement.

column2: What troubleshooting path does this uneven dim-display symptom suggest after brightness and power-management settings are checked?
column4: A possible backlight or panel-lighting problem, especially if only part of the image is dim.
column6:
Troubleshooting Logic

Settings can dim the whole display, but a visible uneven dim area suggests a lighting fault. Check settings first, then escalate with the observed pattern.
